response.justification: While the candidate is a highly accomplished senior engineer with strong fundamentals, they lack the core mandatory requirements for this role. C++ experience is limited to approximately 6 months of professional work in 2014, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on development. Additionally, there is zero exposure to financial services, capital markets, or fixed income domains—and no SQL Server, Oracle, or Windows platform experience. The career trajectory since 2015 has moved firmly toward web/cloud/backend development, suggesting C++ expertise has atrophied significantly.
strengths: Proven senior-level track record at top-tier tech companies (Google, Meta); strong software engineering fundamentals and delivery capability
Excellent communication and mentoring skills demonstrated through workshops, talks, and leadership of open-source projects
Solid experience with CI/CD pipelines, DevOps infrastructure, and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development)
concerns: C++ professional experience limited to ~6 months in 2014; job requires 5–10 years of strong, hands-on C++ development on Windows
Completely missing financial services, capital markets, or fixed income domain expertise—a mandatory requirement for at least one of the two domain tracks
Career has diverged from systems/low-level programming since 2015; primary focus on Node.js, TypeScript, web, and cloud—suggests C++ skills are not current
